_source: Synthetic representative profile for Tsilioni 2015 Transl Psychiatry (PMID 26418275) luteolin-containing formulation OPEN-LABEL trial. Trial cohort was n=38 ASD children; post-hoc bimodal stratification on baseline serum IL-6 + TNF identified a high-cytokine subgroup (n=10) that showed significant VABS improvement (effect sizes 0.35–0.45, p=0.0003 to p=0.008). This representative encodes the high-cytokine subgroup phenotype — the Theoharides MCAS-like inflammatory subset that the formulation was designed to address. Target phenotype PHE-0003 (regressive immune-inflammatory). Cohort-level responder fraction = 10/38 = 0.263.

atopic_cluster._allergic_like_symptoms_per_theoharides_: Tsilioni cites that as many as 50% of ASD children have allergic-like symptoms implicating MC activation

_engine_expectation.rationale: Elevated IL-6 + elevated TNF cytokine pattern drives PHE-0003 (regressive immune-inflammatory) loading via the engine's atlas-driven biomarker shifts. Tsilioni 2015 stratified post-hoc by exactly this cytokine pattern (n=10/38 high-cytokine subgroup); the engine's PHE-0003 loading on this profile should approximate the cohort-level responder fraction (10/38 = 0.263) — testing whether the engine correctly identifies the proportion of an unselected cohort that falls into the inflammatory-responder subset. Open-label trial; numeric agreement at this level is necessarily approximate.
